pattern: A sliding window with two monotonic deques — one for the running maximum and one for the minimum — lets you check the constraint in O(1) per step.
hints: The absolute difference between any two elements in a window equals max(window) - min(window); track both ends with deques.
Expand the right pointer freely; when max - min exceeds the limit, advance the left pointer and evict stale deque entries.
Each element is pushed and popped at most once from each deque, giving O(n) total time.

edge_inputs.description: single element — window of size 1 always valid
limit zero, all same values — entire array is one window
limit zero, mixed values — only single-element windows valid
elements that satisfy limit by value but differ greatly at ends

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(size)]
    limit = rng.randint(0, 1000000000)
    return [nums, limit]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def longestSubarray(self, nums, limit):
        from collections import deque
        # Use sorted container approach: two pointers with SortedList
        # Fallback: O(n log n) with sorted list for small inputs, else skip inner loop early
        best = 1
        n = len(nums)
        l = 0
        window = []
        import bisect
        for r in range(n):
            bisect.insort(window, nums[r])
            while window[-1] - window[0] > limit:
                idx = bisect.bisect_left(window, nums[l])
                window.pop(idx)
                l += 1
            best = max(best, r - l + 1)
        return best
